titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Nicaragua Canal Company Records
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday February 25, 2022 at 04:26

did.unittitle: Nicaragua Canal Company records

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: In English, with a small amount of material in Spanish.

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, financial and legal papers, clippings, and other documents relating to the Nicaragua Canal Company. Included are original charters and agreements with the Nicaraguan government to build a canal, as well as financial statements, partnership agreements between American investors, and lists of stockholders in the Nicaragua Canal Construction Company, such as former United States Vice President Levi P. Morton and a number of senators. In addition, the collection contains correspondence and documents concerning the failure of the project, shareholder lawsuits, allegations the Nicaragua Canal Construction Company mismanaged funds, and disputes between shareholder factions over a plan to reorganize the company.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Waiting for Godot Books on the Walter Jennings Memorial Fund, 2012.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

bioghist.head: Nicaragua Canal Company
bioghist.p: The Nicaragua Canal Company, also known as the Maritime Canal Company of Nicaragua, was chartered by the United States Congress in 1889 to build a transisthmian canal across Nicaragua. The company assumed control from the Nicaragua Canal Association, which had been assigned to the project in 1887. The Nicaragua Canal Construction Company, organized by an act of Congress incorporating the Maritime Canal Company, was tasked with developing local infrastructure for the project and building the canal. Construction began in 1890, and by 1892 the company had completed the final surveys and maps and built a project headquarters at the proposed canal entry at Greytown, Nicaragua, including barracks, a hospital, and warehouses and machine shops. The company also laid down eleven miles of railway tracks, dredged one mile of canal, and cleared 5,000 acres of forest. Amid financing issues and allegations by stockholders that the Nicaragua Canal Construction Company mismanaged funds, the project was suspended in 1893.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Nicaragua Canal Company Records. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

relatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Printed material received with the collection was removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, financial and legal papers, clippings, and other documents relating to the Nicaragua Canal Company. Included are original charters and agreements with the Nicaraguan government to build a canal, as well as financial statements, partnership agreements between American investors, and lists of stockholders in the Nicaragua Canal Construction Company, such as former United States Vice President Levi P. Morton and a number of senators. In addition, the collection contains correspondence and documents concerning the failure of the project, shareholder lawsuits, allegations the Nicaragua Canal Construction Company mismanaged funds, and disputes between shareholder factions over a plan to reorganize the company.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Nicaragua Canal Company Records is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Materials Concerning the History of a Nicaraguan Canal
Hatch, Frederick H. Letter from the Committee in Opposition to the Bartlett Plan of Reorganization
Stevens, C. Amory. Letter to John Barker, Morton Bliss & Co.
Humphreys, Solon. Letter to J. C. Gardener
The Chamber of Commerce of San Francisco memorial regarding Nicaraguan canal construction
Opinions of Messrs. Lourey [Lowry] and Porter
Cuadro de distancias comprendidas entre Barranquilla y Caracoli
Ship - Canal (via Nicaragua) uniting the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans: copy, proposed charter
Revised manuscript for "proposed charter"
Second revised typed manuscript of a charter
Morris, Francis. Letter to Nicaraguan President Pedro Joaquín Chamorro Alfaro
Cox, James. Translation, extract from the Gacete Oficial dated Managua Thursday 20th March 1879
Newspaper clippings about the Panama Canal and proposed Nicaragua Canal
Legal Documents and Correspondence
Draft agreement between Charles H. Stebbins, Philip Tabb, James O'Shaughnessy and Edward R. Johnes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TO form a syndicate for the purposed of investigating the Nicaragua Canal project

did.unittitle: Agreement between Charles H. Stebbins, Philip Tabb, James O'Shaughnessy, Edward R. Johnes, and Robert Bryce, typescript
Agreement between Charles H. Stebbins, Philip Tabb, James O'Shaughnessy, Robert Bryce, and Edward R. Johnes, autograph manuscript
Draft agreement, exhibit A
Agreement and proposed settlement between A. L. Blackman and Charles H. Stebbins
Copy of agreement between Francis A. Stout, Horace L. Hotchkiss, James P. O'Shaughnessy, and Charles H. Stebbins regarding shares of Nicaragua Canal Association

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Related to shares in the Nicaragua Canal Association

did.unittitle: Stebbins, Minnie V. Copy of a letter to President Grover Cleveland
Philip Tabb and Charles H. Stebbins letter and agreement
Unsigned testimony related to Francis C. Morris and Nicaragua Canal rights
Agreement between Frances M. Delprat and Philip Tabb related to the estate of Francis C. Morris
Edward R. Johnes deposition
Signed agreement, copy release between Philip Tabb and John C. F. Gardener
Statement of facts regarding the position of Philip Tabb of Gloucester, Va., and Charles H. Stebbins of New York
Copy order of discontinuance and consent, Philip Tabb against James O'Shaughnessy and the Nicaragua Canal Construction Company
Agreement between E. R. Johnes, Philip Tabb, and Charles H. Stebbins
Daly, Hoyt & Mason. Letter to John C. F. Gardner
Draft of a letter to James O'Shaughnessey concerning Tabb lawsuit
Agreement of extension between Philip Tabb versus Charles H. Stebbins
Notes, drafts of letters, and other papers
Materials Related to Finances and Expenditures of the Nicaragua Canal Co.
Nicaragua Canal Construction Company financial statements
Reasons for desiring to see the books of the Nicaragua Canal Construction Co.
Atkins, Thomas B. Letters to John C. F. Gardner
Waud & Edwards. Receipt to John C. F. Gardner
Miller, J. W. Letter to John C.F. Gardner
1894 Stockholder Documents and Lists
List of stockholders, Nicaragua Canal Co.
Stockholders Nic. Canal Cons. Co.
Stockholders and principal stockholders, including U.S. senators and congressman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Republican politicians including former Vice President Levi P. Morton, Senators James Graham Fair and William Pierce Frye, and Representative Bellamy Storer.

did.unittitle: 1894 Correspondence and Documents Concerning Bartlett Plan of Reorganization
Gardner, John C. F. Letters from Thomas B. Atkins, John R. Bartlett
Telegrams, resolutions, receipts, and other papers
Bradford, Gamaliel. Letter to the editor, New York Times
Downing, Wellington. Letters to Frederick H. Hatch and John C. F. Gardner
Gardner, John C. F. Receipts to Committee of Stockholders of the Nicaragua Canal Construction Company
Gardner, John C. F. Letters from various Nicaragua Canal Construction Company stockholders
Hatch, Frederick H. Letters to John C. F. Gardner and unidentified recipients
Hatch, Frederick H. Letters from various Nicaragua Canal Construction Company stockholders
Mason, Alexander. Letters to John Bartlett and John C. F. Gardner
Miller, J. W. Carbon copy form letters and stock option applications
Stevens, C. Amory. Letters to John C. F. Gardner
Weed, Smith M. Signed carbon copy of form letter to stockholders
Press Notices and News Clippings Related to Bartlett Plan of Reorganization
Newspaper notices and invoices
Clippings
Clippings
